info.title: HowiCC API
info.description: Contract-first API for syncing, storing, profiling, and rendering agent conversations. Browser integrations use Better Auth session cookies, while the CLI uses bearer tokens issued through the CLI auth exchange flow.

tags.name: System
tags.description: Operational endpoints for health and readiness checks.

tags.name: CLI Auth
tags.description: Browser-to-CLI authentication flows that mint bearer tokens for the CLI.

tags.name: API Tokens
tags.description: User-facing API-token CRUD for the settings page. Tokens are minted in plaintext once and stored as SHA-256 hashes; revocation is soft.

tags.name: Uploads
tags.description: Draft upload, asset transfer, and revision finalization endpoints used during sync.

tags.name: Conversations
tags.description: Authenticated read APIs for conversation summaries and render documents.

tags.name: Artifacts
tags.description: Artifact preview endpoints for the current conversation revision.

tags.name: Profiles
tags.description: Personal analytics and materialized session profile endpoints.

tags.name: Public Profile
tags.description: Public profile pages at /:username — opt-in, filtered per-section via publicProfileSettings.

tags.name: Repositories
tags.description: Public aggregate analytics for repositories with shared sessions, plus admin surfaces for GitHub-verified repo admins.

tags.name: Views
tags.description: Fire-and-forget view counters for shared conversations and public profiles. Debounced per viewer per day.

tags.name: Open Graph
tags.description: Server-rendered social cards for public pages.

tags.name: Viewer
tags.description: Cookie-authenticated browser helper and smoke-test endpoints.

BearerAuth.description: CLI bearer token issued by the `/cli-auth/exchange` flow.

BrowserSession.name: __Secure-howicc.session_token
BrowserSession.description: Better Auth browser session cookie. Local HTTP development uses `howicc.session_token` without the `__Secure-` prefix.

secret.description: The plaintext bearer token. Returned once at creation time; the server only stores a SHA-256 hash. The caller must capture this value immediately — it cannot be retrieved later.

blocks.description: Nested render blocks for the delegated subagent thread. The outer document carries the full discriminated block schema.

consentRequired.description: True when the caller must re-acknowledge the private-repo notice before editing. Resets every 30 days and after any visibility change by another admin.

acknowledged.description: Must be `true` — the client asserts the admin clicked the confirmation button.

previewToken.description: Opaque token returned alongside the preview. The caller MUST include it in the subsequent PATCH /visibility request as proof the preview was viewed. The server rejects PATCH requests missing a recent preview token to force the two-step review flow.
Opaque token returned by GET /repo/:owner/:name/preview?target=... The server validates the token matches the requested visibility and is at most 10 minutes old.

get.summary: Health check
get.description: Reports whether the API worker is reachable and ready to serve requests.

post.summary: Create a one-time browser-authenticated CLI login grant
post.description: Creates a short-lived authorization grant for the local CLI login flow. The caller must already have a valid Better Auth browser session cookie.

post.summary: Exchange a one-time CLI auth code for an API token
post.description: Consumes a previously issued one-time CLI grant and returns a bearer token for subsequent CLI API calls.

get.summary: Resolve the current CLI token to the owning user
get.description: Validates the bearer token presented by the CLI and returns the owning user record.

get.summary: List API tokens for the authenticated user
get.description: Returns the caller's API tokens with opaque metadata (id, 12-char prefix, createdAt, revokedAt). Tokens are never returned in plaintext after creation.

post.summary: Mint a new API token for the authenticated user
post.description: Generates a new `hwi_*` bearer token for the caller. The plaintext `secret` is returned once in this response; the server only stores a SHA-256 hash. Pair the new token with the CLI (`howicc login` or a manual config entry) to authenticate subsequent requests.

delete.summary: Revoke an API token owned by the caller
delete.description: Sets `revokedAt` on the caller's token. Revoked tokens remain in the list (marked revoked) so the UI can surface audit history; they stop authenticating immediately. Returns 404 when the token does not exist or belongs to a different user — the 404 is deliberate so non-owners can't probe for token ids.

404.description: Token not found or not owned by the caller

post.summary: Create an upload session for a new revision
post.description: Starts a draft upload session for a conversation revision. The response includes the storage targets that the CLI should upload next.

put.summary: Upload asset bytes for a draft revision session
put.description: Uploads a single asset body for an existing draft upload session. Each asset kind must match a target announced by the initial session creation response.

200.description: Asset bytes stored for the draft upload session

404.description: Upload session or draft asset not found

post.description: Validates the uploaded assets, stores the new revision, and updates the conversation to point at the latest current revision.

get.summary: List conversations for the current user
get.description: Returns the current user's accessible conversations as lightweight list items for navigation and conversation selection.

get.summary: Get the current render document for a conversation
get.description: Returns the normalized render document for the conversation's current revision. The render document powers the viewer UI and downstream presentation layers.

get.summary: Fetch a conversation render document by public slug
get.description: Returns the normalized render document for a conversation identified by its share slug. Private conversations are only visible to the owner (authenticated via CLI bearer token or Better Auth session cookie); unlisted and public conversations are readable without auth. When multiple conversations share the same slug — a historical collision pending schema-level uniqueness — the most-recently-updated match is returned.

404.description: Slug does not resolve to a visible conversation

500.description: Shared render document resolution failed unexpectedly

patch.summary: Update the visibility of a conversation
patch.description: Flips a conversation between `private`, `unlisted`, and `public`. Only the owner may make this change. Accepts either a CLI bearer token or a Better Auth session cookie.

404.description: Conversation does not exist or is not owned by the caller

get.summary: Fetch a text preview for an asset referenced by a render block
get.description: Returns the stored text preview and metadata for an `AssetRef` captured in the canonical session (source file, tool output, plan file, brief attachment, MCP blob). Visibility-gated against the owning conversation: private conversations require the owner bearer token or cookie. Callers that just want the session's render document should use `/shared/:slug` or `/conversations/:id/render` instead.

get.summary: Fetch an artifact payload for a conversation revision
get.description: Returns a text preview for a stored artifact associated with the current conversation revision.

get.summary: Proxy the OpenRouter model catalog
get.description: Returns the latest normalized OpenRouter public pricing catalog snapshot cached by the API.

get.summary: Get the authenticated user profile
get.description: Returns the materialized cross-session profile for the current user. The endpoint accepts either a CLI bearer token or a Better Auth browser session cookie. If the user has not synced any digests yet, the API returns `profile: null` with `digestCount: 0` instead of a 404.

200.description: User profile or an empty state when no synced sessions exist yet

401.description: The caller is not authenticated with either bearer token or browser session cookie

get.summary: Get lightweight stats for the authenticated user
get.description: Returns a compact snapshot of the current user's profile — session count, total cost, total duration, active days, and current streak. Designed for dashboard headers where the full `/profile` payload is too heavy. If no digests exist yet, `stats` is `null` with `digestCount: 0`.

200.description: Profile stats snapshot or an empty state when no synced sessions exist yet

401.description: The caller is not authenticated with either bearer token or browser session cookie

get.summary: Paginated activity feed for the authenticated user
get.description: Returns the authenticated user's synced sessions ordered by session creation time (newest first). Used by the web dashboard feed and the `/sessions` list. Pagination is cursor-based: pass the previous response's `nextCursor` to continue.

schema.description: Pass `nextCursor` from the previous response. ISO 8601 timestamp — items strictly before it are returned.

parameters.description: Pass `nextCursor` from the previous response. ISO 8601 timestamp — items strictly before it are returned.
parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Number of items to return (1–50). Defaults to 20.

parameters.description: Number of items to return (1–50). Defaults to 20.
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Limit the feed to a single visibility tier.

parameters.description: Limit the feed to a single visibility tier.
parameters.name: visibility

schema.description: Case-insensitive substring match against conversation title and project key. Trimmed; ignored when empty.

parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match against conversation title and project key. Trimmed; ignored when empty.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Exact-match repository full name (e.g. `owner/name`) from the session digest.

parameters.description: Exact-match repository full name (e.g. `owner/name`) from the session digest.
parameters.name: repository

200.description: A page of activity items plus an optional `nextCursor` for the next page

401.description: The caller is not authenticated with either bearer token or browser session cookie

post.description: Forces a fresh profile aggregation from the current conversation digests for the authenticated user. This is useful after a sync or when debugging stale profile materializations.

401.description: The caller is not authenticated with either bearer token or browser session cookie

get.summary: Get a public profile by username
get.description: Returns the filtered public aggregate for the requested user. Every optional section respects the owner's visibility flags; disabled sections are omitted from the payload entirely. Returns 404 when the user does not exist or has not opted into a public profile.

schema.description: Lowercased GitHub login. Usernames are the authoritative identifier for public profiles; they mirror GitHub as the source of truth.

parameters.description: Lowercased GitHub login. Usernames are the authoritative identifier for public profiles; they mirror GitHub as the source of truth.
parameters.name: username

404.description: Unknown user or profile is not public

get.summary: Get the caller’s own public-profile configuration
get.description: Returns the authenticated user's public profile configuration so the settings page can render its initial state without guessing. Mirrors the shape returned by PATCH /profile/public-settings.

patch.summary: Update the caller’s public-profile opt-in and settings
patch.description: Updates the authenticated user's public profile configuration: the master enabled flag, optional bio and website, and the six per-section visibility flags. Returns the full canonical settings after the patch so the caller can re-render without a second fetch.

post.summary: Record a view on a public profile
post.description: Fire-and-forget endpoint that bumps the per-user `profile_view_count`. Debounced per IP per day to prevent inflation. Returns `recorded: false` when the view was debounced.

404.description: Unknown user or profile is not public

get.summary: Get the public aggregate profile for a repository
get.description: Aggregates the current public conversation digests for the requested repository across all users. Only conversations whose current revision is publicly visible are included in the result. If the repo is marked `private`, the response omits the profile (sessionCount=0, message set). The `members` tier returns a profile only for viewers who have ≥ read access on GitHub.

200.description: Repository profile or an empty state when no public sessions exist

400.description: The repository owner or name contains unsupported characters

get.summary: Get admin settings for a repository
get.description: Returns repo visibility, hidden-conversation list, and the caller's GitHub permission on the repo. Requires `admin` or `maintain` access verified through the caller's GitHub OAuth token.

403.description: Caller lacks admin/maintain permission on the repo

get.summary: Check whether the admin must re-acknowledge the private-repo notice
get.description: Returns whether the caller must re-acknowledge the private-repo notice before editing this repo's settings. A fresh consent is required when the repo is private AND the caller has not consented within the last 30 days, or whenever another admin changed the visibility since the caller's last consent.

403.description: Caller lacks admin/maintain permission on the repo

post.description: Records the caller's acknowledgement of the private-repo notice. The acknowledgement unlocks other admin endpoints (visibility change, hide/unhide) for 30 days, or until another admin changes visibility.

403.description: Caller lacks admin/maintain permission on the repo

get.summary: Preview which conversations would be aggregated at a target visibility
get.description: Lists the public/unlisted conversations that would appear on the repo page if visibility were changed to `target`. Returns an opaque `previewToken` that must accompany the subsequent PATCH /visibility request to prove the preview was reviewed. Prevents accidental one-click exposure of private aggregation.

allOf.description: The visibility tier the caller is considering.

parameters.description: The visibility tier the caller is considering.
parameters.name: target

403.description: Caller lacks admin/maintain permission on the repo

patch.description: Flips the repo visibility tier. Requires admin consent (POST /consent within 24h) AND a valid preview token (GET /preview within 10 min). Individual conversation visibility is NOT affected — this only changes the aggregation ceiling.

400.description: Invalid or stale preview token, or missing/stale consent

403.description: Caller lacks admin/maintain permission on the repo

post.summary: Hide a conversation from the repo aggregation
post.description: Excludes the conversation from `/r/:owner/:name` stats without touching the conversation's own visibility — the owner can still share the `/s/:slug` link directly. Requires admin consent. Idempotent: a repeat call refreshes `hiddenAt`.

schema.description: Conversation id to hide or unhide from repo aggregation.

parameters.description: Conversation id to hide or unhide from repo aggregation.
parameters.name: conversationId

403.description: Caller lacks admin/maintain permission on the repo

404.description: Conversation does not exist or is not linked to this repo

delete.summary: Unhide a conversation from the repo aggregation
delete.description: Removes a conversation from the hidden-from-aggregation set so it reappears in `/r/:owner/:name` stats. Requires admin consent.

schema.description: Conversation id to hide or unhide from repo aggregation.

parameters.description: Conversation id to hide or unhide from repo aggregation.
parameters.name: conversationId

403.description: Caller lacks admin/maintain permission on the repo

get.summary: Get the current browser viewer session
get.description: Returns a safe summary of the current Better Auth browser session. This endpoint is intended for SSR helpers, auth debugging, and browser-only integrations that need to confirm whether a session cookie is active.

200.description: Current viewer session summary or an unauthenticated empty state

get.summary: Load the protected viewer smoke-test page
get.description: Returns a simple HTML page when the current browser session is authenticated. This route exists to smoke-test cookie-based auth from the web app and local environments.

200.description: Protected HTML content rendered for an authenticated browser session

401.description: The browser session is missing or invalid

post.summary: Record a view on a shared conversation
post.description: Fire-and-forget endpoint bumped by the public `/s/:slug` page when a non-owner views the conversation. Debounced per IP per conversation per day to prevent inflation. Returns the updated `viewCount` for the conversation and whether the view was counted (`recorded`).

404.description: Unknown conversation or not publicly accessible
